topics: Introduction to Signals and Systems, Elementary Signals, Classification and representation of continuous time and discrete time signals, Signal operations. Concept of system, Continuous time and discrete time systems
Properties of systems, Time invariance, Linearity, Causality, Systems with and without memory, Stability. Convolution Integral and sum. Impulse and step response
Frequency domain characterization of Signals and Systems, Fourier transform, Existence - Properties of Continuous time Fourier transform

Significance of Fourier transform and difference from Fourier series. Review of Laplace Transforms. Characterization of LTI systems, Differential equation representation of continuous time LTI systems. Transfer function representation of differential equation in Laplace domain. Modeling of LTI systems, Electrical, translational and rotational mechanical systems, DC servo-motor
Sampled Data Systems and Z-Transform, Sampling process
Impulse train sampling-sampling theorem- Aliasing effect. Zero-order and First-order hold circuits

Signal reconstruction. Z-Transform, Region of convergence- Properties of Z-Transform Inverse Z-Transform. Pulse transfer function. Difference equations representation using Z-transform and it’s solution using inverse Z-Transform. Impulse and step response of discrete-time systems
Dynamic System Representation and Stability, Open loop and closed loop systems. Effect of feedback in systems. Block diagram representation
block diagram reduction. Signal flow graph -Mason's gain formula. Type and Order of the systems
Pole-Zero representation of systems. Characteristic equation. Routh stability criterion
